evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Own data correctness and investigate quality anomalies; AI-native workflows; help establish organisational adoption patterns for AI-assisted engineering
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as mixed because rigour signals ('own data correctness', 'quality gates') are balanced by velocity signals ('AI-native workflows', agentic standards for a new mobile game) with genuine equal weight.
evidence.domain_risk: building analytics infrastructure for a new mobile game
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as moderate because mobile gaming analytics affects product decisions and feature design without regulatory or direct financial reporting consequence.
evidence.data_team_maturity: help establish organisational adoption patterns for AI-assisted engineering
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as early because the analytics infrastructure is being built for a new mobile game from scratch and the candidate is tasked with establishing AI adoption patterns, implying no existing framework.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design end-to-end gameplay analytics architecture covering tracking, storage, processing, and reporting; Review AI-generated code on critical paths before production deployment
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as hiring_manager because the JD names specific technical responsibilities (gameplay analytics architecture, agentic workflow oversight, multi-agent prompt management) that clearly describe the Tuesday morning work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support product and design teams with actionable recommendations derived from complex datasets
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as product because the primary beneficiaries are the product and design teams, and the role's output is product intelligence from gameplay data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own data correctness; help establish organisational adoption patterns for AI-assisted engineering
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as strategic because 'own data correctness' and setting 'organisational adoption patterns' signal that the candidate defines standards and direction, not just executes.
evidence.ai_role: Demonstrated expertise using LLMs and agentic workflows in production
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Classified as ai_user because the candidate is expected to demonstrate 'expertise using LLMs and agentic workflows in production' — AI tools accelerate the candidate's own analytics and engineering work.
evidence.testing_framing: Own data correctness and investigate quality anomalies; review processes and quality gates
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as responsibility because 'own data correctness' and establishing 'review processes and quality gates' frame testing and data quality as accountabilities the candidate defines and owns.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Review AI-generated code on critical paths before production deployment
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as moderate because 'review AI-generated code on critical paths before production deployment' signals operational reliability concern, but the JD is primarily delivery-oriented for a new game build.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: building analytics infrastructure for a new mobile game
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as greenfield because the entire analytics architecture is being designed and built from scratch for a new game.
evidence.loss_aversion: Review AI-generated code on critical paths before production deployment — operational reliability concern for AI-generated outputs, not compliance-driven.
